summary: Closed, unmerged PR touching only src/melee/gr/grzebesroute.c. It translated the Zebes Route stage light setup routine grZebesRoute_8020B548 to near-match, named the route light data as a typed aggregate, added direct data/string references, and improved grZebesRoute_8020B260 plus data sections according to the bot report. The PR body reports configure/ninja success and checkdiff for grZebesRoute_8020B548 at 99.96923%, with remaining drift in three GXColor by-value stack temp offsets.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; adds Zebes Route light data typing, direct data/string refs, and implementation of grZebesRoute_8020B548.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: 'naming the route light data, direct string/data references, and translating the stage light setup routine'

what_changed.text: Added baselib/debug.h and baselib/lobj.h includes to support asserts and HSD_LObj light object APIs used by the translated lighting routine.
Replaced the prior extern Vec3 grZe_Route_803B83A0 with a typed const grZebesRoute_LightData aggregate containing player, spot, spot-interest, upper-point, and lower-point Vec3 fields plus padding.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: typedef struct grZebesRoute_LightData with player_pos, spot_pos, spot_interest, upper_point_pos, lower_point_pos, pad

what_changed.text: Added direct data/string symbols for /GrNZr.dat, stage info struct contents, grzebesroute.c filename, assert strings, GXColor constants, and float constants used by stage positioning and lighting.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grZe_Route_803E5E0C stage info struct with callbacks and fmt string

what_changed.text: Adjusted grZebesRoute_8020B260 OSReport to use the newly named stage-info format and filename data, which the bot report says completed that function.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: OSReport changed from literal __FILE__ form to grZe_Route_803E5E0C.fmt and grZe_Route_803E5E64
text_corpus issue_comment: grZebesRoute_8020B260 improved from 99.97% to 100.00%

what_changed.text: Adjusted grZebesRoute_8020B42C to initialize from grZe_Route_803B83A0.player_pos and use named float constants for the y clamp and x reset.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pos.y compared and assigned with grZe_Route_804DB918; pos.x assigned grZe_Route_804DB91C

what_changed.text: Implemented grZebesRoute_8020B548 as a light setup routine that walks up to three HSD_LObj entries after the first light object, classifies lights by LOBJ_TYPE_MASK, and configures spot, upper point, and lower point lights with scaled positions, colors, spot parameters, and distance attenuation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: branches for LOBJ_SPOT and LOBJ_POINT with current_pos.y > split_y or < split_y
human_pr_text: 'translating the stage light setup routine'

smart_moves.text: Using a local typed aggregate for grZe_Route_803B83A0 made the formerly single Vec3 data symbol express several distinct light-related positions, which allowed call sites to use named fields instead of raw offset-style access.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grZebesRoute_LightData fields and field accesses in grZebesRoute_8020B42C and grZebesRoute_8020B548

smart_moves.text: The light setup translation keeps the original base light object path clear: it reads HSD_GObjGXLinkHead[4], asserts if absent, takes first_lobj from hsd_obj, then starts iteration at HSD_LObjGetNext(first_lobj).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: light_gobj = HSD_GObjGXLinkHead[4]; first_lobj = light_gobj->hsd_obj; lobj = HSD_LObjGetNext(first_lobj)

smart_moves.text: The PR body explicitly documented the remaining near-match drift instead of presenting grZebesRoute_8020B548 as matched.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: known remaining drift in three GXColor by-value stack temp offsets

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a stage data blob is reused as several related Vec3 values, recovering a small typed aggregate can make later lighting and position code much more reviewable than treating the symbol as one Vec3.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grZe_Route_803B83A0.player_pos, spot_pos, spot_interest, upper_point_pos, lower_point_pos accesses

decomp_lessons.lesson: For translated HSD_LObj stage lighting routines, classifying lobj->flags with LOBJ_TYPE_MASK and then using HSD_LObjSetColor, HSD_LObjSetPosition, HSD_LObjSetInterest, HSD_LObjSetSpot, and HSD_LObjSetDistAttn can produce a high-score near-match.

Remaining stack-layout drift should be stated narrowly: here the author identified only three GXColor by-value stack temp offsets as the known mismatch while noting that surrounding control flow and Vec3 stack slots were aligned.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: known remaining drift in GXColor stack temp offsets 8/12/16 vs target 20/24/28

naming_conventions.name: grZebesRoute_LightData
naming_conventions.convention: Stage-specific Pascal/Camel mixed typedef names the owning stage and semantic data role.

naming_conventions.name: player_pos, spot_pos, spot_interest, upper_point_pos, lower_point_pos
naming_conventions.convention: Use role-specific Vec3 field names for light/player position data once the role is evidenced by use in stage movement and lighting setup.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: field use in grZebesRoute_8020B42C and grZebesRoute_8020B548

naming_conventions.name: light_gobj, first_lobj, lobj, scale, spot_ref_dist, upper_ref_dist, lower_ref_dist, split_y
naming_conventions.convention: Recovered locals use short semantic role names for stage lighting traversal and attenuation calculations.

naming_conventions.name: grZe_Route_80xxxxxx
naming_conventions.convention: New data symbols retain address-style stage-prefixed names rather than speculative global semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grZe_Route_803E5E00, grZe_Route_803E5E0C, grZe_Route_804DB920 and related constants

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use typed aggregate field loads from grZe_Route_803B83A0 to recover the distinct Vec3 initializers used by player and light setup logic.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: accesses to player_pos, spot_pos, spot_interest, upper_point_pos, lower_point_pos

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a bounded while loop over HSD_LObjGetNext with both an index limit and null check for the three configurable lights.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: i = 0; while (i < 3 && lobj != NULL); i++; lobj = HSD_LObjGetNext(lobj)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Classify point lights using their current y position relative to a split_y constant to choose upper versus lower point-light setup.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: LOBJ_POINT && current_pos.y > split_y and LOBJ_POINT && current_pos.y < split_y branches

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: The attempted match includes a narrow stack-shaping artifact, int pad[3], in the lower point-light branch; PR evidence only supports it as part of a near-match attempt, not as general style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: int pad[3] in lower point branch
human_pr_text: remaining GXColor by-value stack temp offset drift

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported matched code +0.01% (+232 bytes), matched data +0.03% (+328 bytes), five new matches in grzebesroute data sections, grZebesRoute_8020B260 reaching 100%, and grZebesRoute_8020B548 improving from 0.00% to 99.97%.
Author-reported verification: python configure.py && ninja; checkdiff grZebesRoute_8020B548 --no-tty --format json -> 99.96923%.
No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: HSD_LObj stage lighting LOBJ_TYPE_MASK LOBJ_SPOT LOBJ_POINT
int pad[3] GXColor by-value stack temp decomp

notes: The slice includes full diff text and author-reported verification, enough to describe the changed file and tactics.
The PR is closed with merged_at null, so this intake must not imply acceptance or merge.
No human review comments are available; reviewer intent cannot be inferred.
The claimed grZebesRoute_8020B548 result is a high-score near-match, not a full match.
Several literal/data-symbol changes are present; PR evidence and bot report support matching impact, but this intake does not establish a global standard for replacing literals with data symbols.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed aggregate recovery can clarify reused stage light data
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2494, grZe_Route_803B83A0 was recovered as grZebesRoute_LightData with named Vec3 fields for player, spot, spot-interest, upper-point, and lower-point positions. This made grZebesRoute_8020B42C and grZebesRoute_8020B548 use field accesses rather than treating the symbol as a single Vec3.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grZebesRoute_LightData definition and field uses
human_pr_text: naming the route light data

accepted_candidate_records.title: Document near-match drift at the exact stack-object level
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2494 reports grZebesRoute_8020B548 at 99.96923% and identifies the remaining mismatch as three GXColor by-value stack temp offsets, while noting that surrounding control flow and Vec3 stack slots are aligned.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Verification and Known remaining drift sections

source_update_candidates.title: src/melee/gr/grzebesroute.c owns Zebes Route stage lighting setup work
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2494 evidence shows src/melee/gr/grzebesroute.c containing grZebesRoute_8020B548, a translated Zebes Route stage light setup routine using HSD_LObj APIs and route light data.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path-scoped fact for future search; curator should verify against current source before adding.

source_update_candidates.title: Near-match PRs should explicitly record remaining stack-layout drift
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding guidance that when a function is intentionally left as a near-match, the PR should name the exact remaining drift, such as stack temp offsets, rather than only reporting a percentage.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: grZebesRoute_8020B548 checkdiff 99.96923% and GXColor stack temp offset drift
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR provides a concrete example of useful drift reporting, but curator should decide whether it belongs in global standards or is already covered by verification-ledger guidance.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize this PR into a rule that string literals or float constants should be replaced with address-style data symbols; loaded standards warn that literal/data ownership needs evidence and PR #2494 is a single closed, unmerged slice.
Do not treat int pad[3] as accepted style; evidence only shows it appears in the near-matching lower point-light branch while remaining stack drift still exists.
Do not claim grZebesRoute_8020B548 matched; the author and bot report only support approximately 99.97%.
